title: George Stacy
content:
{{Character |name = George Stacy |image= George Stacy TASM.webp |realname = George Stacy |aka = '''Codenames'''
Captain Stacy |species = [[Humans|Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA|}} |gender = Male |job = Captain of the [[New York Police Department]] |DOB = |DOD =April 2013 |status = Deceased |films = ''[[The Amazing Spider-Man]]''
''[[The Amazing Spider-Man 2]]'' (hallucination)
''[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse]]'' (archive cameo) |games = |comics =''[[The Amazing Spider-Man: The Movie]]''
''The Amazing Spider-Man: The Movie Adaptation'' |portrayer = [[Denis Leary]] |voice = |first=''The Amazing Spider-Man: The Movie'' #2|creator=[[James Vanderbilt]], [[Alvin Sargent]], [[Steve Kloves]]|universe=[[Earth-120703]]}} {{Quote|I was wrong about you, [[Spider-Man|Peter]]. This [[New York City|city]] needs you. Here. You're gonna need [[Spider-Man Suit|this]]. You're gonna make [[Electro|en]][[Green Goblin|em]][[Rhino|ies]]. People will get hurt. Sometimes the [[May Parker|people]] [[Gwendolyne Stacy|closest to you]]. So I want you to promise me something, okay? Leave Gwen out of it. [[George Stacy and Peter Parker's Promise|Promise me that]]. Huh? You promise me.|George Stacy to [[Spider-Man]], foreshadowing the events of ''[[The Amazing Spider-Man 2]]''|The Amazing Spider-Man}} Captain '''George Stacy''' was a captain in the [[New York Police Department]] and the father of [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]]. After the emergence of [[Spider-Man]], George considered him a dangerous anarchist. This drew him and the NYPD into conflict with the masked vigilante. After discovering Spider-Man's identity as Peter Parker, he assisted him in battling the [[Lizard]], but was killed by the monster itself. Moments before death, he told Peter to keep Gwen far away from the danger of being Spider-Man. ==Biography== === Early Life === An esteemed police officer for over twenty years, Captain Stacy is the public face of the [[New York City Police Department|NYPD]]. The only thing Captain Stacy vows to protect more than the city is his family, and particularly his only daughter, [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]], assistant to [[Lizard|Curt Connors]] in [[Oscorp]].[[The Amazing Spider-Man|''The Amazing Spider-Man'']] ===Masked Vigilante=== When [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]] begins hunting down [[Cash register thief|Uncle Ben's killer]] and making himself known to the public, with most of the potential culprits not having a star tattoo on their wrist, Stacy immediately dislikes the mysterious costumed crime fighter. He then began a manhunt on him. ====Family Dinner with Parker==== [[File:Peter with Stacies (ASM1).png|left|thumb|248x248px|Stacy and [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]] discuss [[Spider-Man]]]]{{Quote|I'm saying [[Spider-Man|he]]'s trying to help and it looks like he's trying to do something what the [[NYPD|police]] can't."
"Something the police can't?"
"I don't know."
"What do you think we do up there? You think we're just sitting around eating doughnuts with our thumbs planted firmly up our asses?|[[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]] and George Stacy|The Amazing Spider-Man}} Later, Captain Stacy opens the door to [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]]'s room and finds her talking to [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]], whom she had invited to her house for dinner earlier that day. Although they weren't doing anything but talking, Stacy obviously isn't thrilled about the picture and becomes suspicious about Parker. At dinner, when the topic of Spider-Man comes up, Stacy discusses with his family and Peter about how he will capture the masked menace; Peter defends [[Spider-Man]] as "doing something the police can't", and begins to accidentally anger Stacy, further ruining his first impression. Or it’s possible that Captain Stacy thought Peter was being a Smart Ass to him when talk about spider man defending the city when the police can’t. Do not revert again. ==== [[Confrontation at Williamsburg Bridge]] ==== {{Quote|Which is why, this morning, I am issuing an arrest warrant for the masked vigilante known as [[Spider-Man]].|George Stacy|The Amazing Spider-Man}} However, dinner is suddenly interrupted when [[Lizard|The Lizard]] is spotted [[Confrontation at Williamsburg Bridge|rampaging]] through the [[Williamsburg Bridge]], and Stacy immediately goes out to hunt down the monster. After [[Spider-Man]] saves the lives of several people and fights off the Lizard on the bridge, Stacy's hatred toward Spider-Man remains undeterred, and the next morning, he issues an [[Hunt for Spider-Man|arrest warrant]] for "the masked vigilante known as 'Spider-Man'." He continually gets New York's finest police officers to go after him but to no avail. ==== Learning about Lizard ==== [[File:George and Peter talking about Connors.png|left|thumb|250x250px|Stacy obtaining information about the [[The Lizard Rampage in Manhattan|rampage]] by [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]]]] {{Quote|You know, recently, Dr. [[Lizard|Connors]] gave [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]] a glowing college recommendation. It was beautiful. I read it, I cried. But you would have me believe that he is [[The Lizard Rampage in Manhattan|running around]] dressed up like a giant dinosaur?"
"Not dressing up, not a dinosaur. He has [[Lizard Serum|transformed]] himself into a giant lizard."
"Let me ask you a question. Do I look like the mayor of {{WPS|Tokyo}} to you?|George Stacy and [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}}After heading back to his office at the [[New York City Police Department|NYPD]]'s headquarters, [[Spider-Man|Peter]] arrives there and tells him that Dr. [[Lizard|Curt Connors]] is going to [[The Lizard Rampage in Manhattan|turn]] all of [[New York]] into powerful [[Cross-Species Hybrids|man-lizards]] and that Stacy has to help him stop him. However, Stacy, who was already disapproving of Peter and thought he was a stupid head from the night before and very gruffy towards him, has the cops escort him out of the building, refusing to believe what Peter says. However, after contemplating it, he reluctantly has an officer run everything they have on file about Connors, suggesting Parker's statement to be possibly true. Stacy would arrive at the [[Midtown Science High School]], where a [[Battle of Midtown High|confrontation]] between the masked vigilante and the Lizard was taken place at. After helping clean up the [[Battle of Midtown High|battle]] between [[Spider-Man]] and The [[Lizard]] at the school, Stacy resumes the hunt for both of them. ====Finding Out Spider-Man's Identity==== [[File:Captain Stacy aims a gun.jpg|thumb|237x237px|Captain Stacy pointing his gun at [[Spider-Man]]]]{{Quote|Your [[Spider-Man|boyfriend]] is a man of many masks. I get it.|George Stacy to [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}} That night, [[Lizard]] launches a [[Battle at Oscorp Tower|biological attack]] on [[New York City]], and while Stacy orders a squad to stop it, Stacy himself instead personally leads a chase on [[Spider-Man]]. When Spider-Man is caught in the sight of the police, Stacy's helicopter stuns Spider-Man out of the air. [[File:George and Spider-Man (Peter).png|left|thumb|250x250px|George after seeing [[Spider-Man]] unmask himself]] The cops put Spider-Man in handcuffs, but when Stacy pulls off his mask, Spider-Man quickly beats up the cops and is about to escape until Stacy gets up and holds him at gunpoint, forcing Spider-Man to freeze. Realizing the situation, Spider-Man turns around and reveals himself to be [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]]. Stunned, Stacy is taken back by the revelation, but Peter convinces him that he has to stop the Lizard before it reaches Oscorp where Gwen is, and Stacy reluctantly lets him go. He then heads over to [[Oscorp Industries|Oscorp]], where he runs into Gwen. He reveals to her that he knows about her boyfriend's secret, and she gives him an [[Reptillian Antidote|antidote]] that will cure the [[Lizard Serum|lizard toxin]] and pleads him to make sure Peter's okay. ====[[Battle at Oscorp Tower|Death]]==== [[File:He's not alone.png|thumb|250x250px|Stacy after shooting at the [[Lizard]]]] {{Quote|I got this, [[Spider-Man|Peter]]!|George Stacy to [[Spider-Man]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}}Eventually, Stacy somehow makes it near the top of [[Oscorp Tower]] and assists [[Spider-Man]] in defeating the [[Lizard]]. He gives Peter the [[Reptillian Antidote|antidote]] from [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]] and buys him time by fighting the Lizard himself. While Peter goes to switch the [[Lizard Serum|lizard toxin]] with the antidote, Stacy fires his shotgun at Lizard, but the monstrous reptile quickly heals from his wounds and impales the Captain, mortally wounding him. Peter successfully releases the antidote into the air, but falls from the collapsing tower, and is saved by [[Lizard|Connors]], who is beginning to transform back to his [[Humans|normal]] form. [[File:George Stacy dying.png|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter Parker and Captain Stacy decided to keep a promise to leave Stacy's daugther, Gwen, out of Peter's vigilantism, before the cop's death.]] Peter returns to Stacy, who is bleeding heavily. In his dying moments, Stacy tells Peter that he was wrong and that [[New York]] does need Spider-Man. However, he warns Peter that being a superhero will often put the ones closest to him in danger and that it will come with sacrifices. He then forces Peter to promise that he will leave Gwen out of it, so that she is not harmed by Peter's status as Spider-Man. Peter agrees, and mourns as George Stacy dies. ===Legacy=== ====Funeral==== Later, Stacy has a funeral where he is honored for his work as New York's top cop, and Gwen notes that everyone, even [[Flash Thompson|Flash]], shows up, except for Peter. She arrives at his apartment, and Peter tells her that he can no longer be with her, and she realizes that her father made him promise that. The next day in school, the two sadly ignore each other, until, in class, the teacher scolds Peter for being late, and when he says it won't happen again, she tells him not to make promises he cannot keep. Sitting behind Gwen, Peter quietly and slyly replies, "Yeah, but those are the best kind," which causes Gwen to smile, meaning that, for better or worse, Peter is going to stop honoring his promise to Captain Stacy and start dating Gwen again. ====Peter's Hallucination==== George Stacy appears often as a vision and hallucination to [[Spider-Man]], haunting him for the promise he did not keep and does not keep about his daughter. Gwen's death at the hands of the [[Green Goblin]] proved that Stacy was right because Peter did not keep his distance from her.[[The Amazing Spider-Man 2|''The Amazing Spider-Man 2'']] ====Monitored across the Spider-Verse==== [[File:The Death of George Stacy (Earth-120703).png|thumb|251x251px|"The Death of George Stacy"]] As [[Spider-Man 2099]] was teaching [[Miles Morales]] about the concept of a Canon Event, and how it is a constant throughout each universe within the Spider-Verse, footage of [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]] was presented that showed him at the death of George Stacy and how his death is a Canon Event.[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse|''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'']] ==Personality== {{Quote|Also, my father is under the impression you require psychiatric attention.|[[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]] to [[Spider-Man|Peter Parker]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}} ''To be added'' ==Equipment== *'''SIG-Sauer P226 DAK:''' Captain Stacy held [[Spider-Man]] at gunpoint with his sidearm before letting him go to fight the [[Lizard]]. *'''Heckler & Koch FABARM:''' Captain Stacy attacked the [[Lizard]] with his shotgun to aid [[Spider-Man]]. ==Relationships== {{Section stub}} ===Family=== *[[Helen Stacy]] - Wife *[[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]] † - Daughter *[[Philip Stacy]] - Son *[[Howard Stacy]] - Son *[[Simon Stacy]] - Son ===Allies=== *[[NYPD|New York Police Department]] - Subordinates *[[Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] - Former Enemy ===Enemies=== *[[Lizard|Curtis Connors/Lizard]] - Attempted Victim and Killer ==Trivia== *The age of George Stacy differs largely from his comic book counterpart. In the movie, he was a middle-aged man whereas he was a grey haired old man in the comic. *George Stacy was one of the few people to know Spider-Man's true identity before he died. The others are his daughter, [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]], and [[Lizard|Curt Connors]]. ** Both how George Stacy discovers Peter's identity was changed heavily from the comics. In the comics, he doesn't actually say how he learnt that Peter was Spider-Man (Peter just commenting "he's always known") but it was implied that he simply used his deductive skills and deduced it using his absences. In the movie, Peter unmasks to save himself from getting shot and/or arrested. This was presumably changed because it was an unresolved loose end in the comics as to how he learnt Peter's identity. ***As well as this, the place where he discovered it is changed. In the comics, the place is unclear (likely the police station though he doesn't reveal it until he is dying) whereas he learns it in an alley in the movie. ***George Stacy is a cop, he was by [[Electro]] after, in the police car. ***He is also one of the first iteration to disapprove Spider-Man at first, before finding out his true identity and teaming up with him. ==Behind the Scenes== ''To be added'' ==References== {{Gallery|George Stacy/Gallery}} {{Wq|George Stacy}} {{Reflist}} ==Links== *{{Marvel|George Stacy}} *{{WP|George Stacy}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Male]] [[Category:Heroes]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man characters]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man 2 characters]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man (video game) Characters]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (video game) Characters]] [[Category:New York City Police Department]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man characters]] [[Category:Keeper of Peter Parker's secret identity]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]]
